title: Epithecia oerstedii

It is classified as a terrestrial orchid, growing in tropical and subtropical regions. As a member of the Orchidaceae family, Epithecia oerstedii is closely related to other orchid species. The classification of Epithecia oerstedii is further broken down into the order Asparagales and the class Liliopsida. The kingdom to which Epithecia oerstedii belongs is Plantae.

The plant is known for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Epithecia oerstedii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
